excerpt: Breaking into Riftbound doesn't require breaking the bank.

- **Ultra-Budget ($20-30):** Champion Deck + a few singles - **Budget ($40-60):** Optimized single or dual-domain builds using commons/uncommons - **Budget-Competitive ($80-100):** Tournament-viable decks with some rare/epic inclusions - **Premium ($120+):** Full competitive list
Most players start with a Champion Deck and upgrade gradually.
**Why Start Here:** Sivir, Mercenary (SFD-143/221) brings aggressive tempo and equipment synergies that scale well with budget upgrades.
**Core Strategy:** Sivir's [Accelerate] ability lets you pay 1 energy + 1 Chaos rune to have her enter ready, immediately threatening battlefields.

**Out-of-the-Box Strengths:** - Strong common base with Treasure Hunter (SFD-130/221) generating Gold tokens - Natural equipment synergies from Spiritforged commons - Card selection spells like Stacked Deck (OGN-183/298) improve consistency - Mobility gameplay teaches positioning
**Immediate Upgrade Path ($10-15):** - 3x Treasure Trove (OGN-186/298) - Uncommon - ~$3-4 total - 2x Switcheroo (SFD-145/221) - Rare - ~$6-8 total - Additional Chaos domain removal spells
**Why Start Here:** Soraka, Wanderer (SFD-173/221) offers a defensive control strategy with built-in unit protection.
**Core Strategy:** Soraka must be assigned combat damage last and can save other units from death by healing, exhausting, and recalling them if they have less Might than her.

**Out-of-the-Box Strengths:** - Solari Shieldbearer (OGN-051/298) and stun effects control tempo - Revival and resurrection effects enable grindy matchups - Spectral Matron (OGN-226/298) brings back units from trash - Teaches resource management and defensive timing
